short_description.value: GeoLangBind is an agglomerative vision?language foundation model for Earth Observation (EO) that unifies heterogeneous EO data modalities (RGB, SAR, multispectral, hyperspectral, infrared, elevation, aerial) into a shared language embedding space. It leverages a large-scale multimodal image?text dataset (GeoLangBind-2M, 2M pairs, 6 modalities), a wavelength-aware dynamic encoder, a modality-aware knowledge agglomeration (MaKA) module, and progressive multimodal weight merging. GeoLangBind achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot and fine-tuned performance on classification, segmentation, and cross-modal retrieval across 23 EO datasets.

backbone.value: ViT-Base (patch size 16) for base, SoViT-400m (patch size 14) for large

pretext_training_type.value: Contrastive vision?language pretraining with multi-teacher knowledge distillation

masking_strategy.value: Progressive multimodal weight merging; dynamic patch embedding with wavelength-aware masking

pretraining.value: Initialized with SigLIP weights, trained on GeoLangBind-2M using pairwise sigmoid loss and multi-teacher feature-matching distillation (SigLIP, DINOv2, ViT)

value.value: Global (datasets cover diverse geographies, e.g., Sentinel-2, Sentinel-1, EnMAP, aerial, elevation, infrared)

sampling.value: Dataset split into RGB and non-RGB modalities for progressive weight merging
